Pick our next swim trip 🏊

Which one, boys?

Two swimming spots, both about an hour from home. Look at the pictures and pick — Dad'll drive. 👇

VS
The lake beach 🚗 ~1 hr

tap a photo to see it big · last one's a classic old park postcard

Cacapon Lake Beach

A real sandy beach on a calm mountain lake — near Berkeley Springs, WV.

  • 🏖️Soft sand beach with a roped-off swim area
  • 🛟Lifeguards on duty — calm, guarded water
  • 🚣Rent a paddle boat, rowboat or kayak
  • 🏰Room to dig, build castles, and lay out a towel
Best if you want: to build sandcastles, paddle a boat around, and swim where it's calm and someone's watching.

ℹ️Open Tue–Sun (closed Mondays), 10:30–5:30 · cash only, about $6 · bring a towel!

◈ Map
The wild swimming hole 🚗 ~1 hr

tap a photo to see it big

Blue Hole on Passage Creek

A wild emerald creek pool in the forest — with a rope swing. Near Front Royal, VA.

  • 💚Deep, clear green pool in a shady creek
  • 🪢A rope swing hangs from a tree over the water
  • 🪨Rocks to climb and splash across
  • 🌲Feels like a secret spot in the woods — and it's free
Best if you want: an adventure — swing on the rope, hop across the rocks, and swim in cold, clear water.

⚠️Wild creek — no lifeguard, so stick close to Dad · water's chilly · bumpy mountain road at the end.

◈ Map

Show Dad your pick and we'll go! (Tap the other one if you change your mind.)

Dad's quick take 👀

Cacapon is the easy, comfy day: a guarded sand beach, paddle boats, and calm water where the little guy can splash safe. Pack sunscreen and a few bucks cash — and remember it's closed Mondays.

Blue Hole is the adventure day: a gorgeous green creek pool with a rope swing and rocks to explore. It's free and feels wild — but there's no lifeguard and the water's cold, so we'd all stick together. Last stretch is a winding mountain road.

Both are about an hour. Can't lose either way — so it's your call, boys.

Photos: Cacapon beach & lake — DiscoverBerkeleySprings.com & Wikimedia Commons; vintage postcard — Boston Public Library (public domain). Passage Creek / Blue Hole — Wikimedia Commons (CC BY-SA). A private page just for us.
